case.title: Batch Synthesis of 3,5-Disubstituted Isoxazole in TBAB/EG (1:5) DES
case.protocol: Phenylacetylene (1a, 0.5 mmol, 1.0 equiv) and ethyl nitroacetate (2, 1.0 mmol, 2.0 equiv) were added directly to TBAB/EG (1:5) DES (1 mL) in an oven-dried 30 mL vial equipped with a magnetic stirring bar; no additional solvent, catalyst, base, or additive was used. The reaction mixture was stirred at 120 °C in an oil bath for 15 min, at which point full conversion (99%) to ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate (3a) was achieved via 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of the in-situ-generated nitrile oxide intermediate with phenylacetylene. After the reaction, the mixture was quenched with water, extracted with dichloromethane, dried over MgSO₄, and the solvent removed under reduced pressure; the isolated NMR yield was 83% (quantified against 1,3,5-trimethoxybenzene as internal standard).
case.notes: User-provided full protocol with 15 min batch time, 83% isolated yield, openai council benchmark.

batch_record.reaction_description: 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of phenylacetylene with ethyl nitroacetate to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
batch_record.raw_text: Phenylacetylene (1a, 0.5 mmol, 1.0 equiv) and ethyl nitroacetate (2, 1.0 mmol, 2.0 equiv) were added directly to TBAB/EG (1:5) DES (1 mL) in an oven-dried 30 mL vial equipped with a magnetic stirring bar; no additional solvent, catalyst, base, or additive was used. The reaction mixture was stirred at 120 °C in an oil bath for 15 min, at which point full conversion (99%) to ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate (3a) was achieved via 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition of the in-situ-generated nitrile oxide intermediate with phenylacetylene. After the reaction, the mixture was quenched with water, extracted with dichloromethane, dried over MgSO₄, and the solvent removed under reduced pressure; the isolated NMR yield was 83% (quantified against 1,3,5-trimethoxybenzene as internal standard).

chemistry_plan.reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of phenylacetylene with ethyl nitroacetate to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
chemistry_plan.bond_formed: C-O and C-C bonds in isoxazole ring
chemistry_plan.bond_broken: N-O bond (loss of water from nitrile oxide precursor)

reagents: phenylacetylene (1.0 equiv, 0.5 M in TBAB/EG 1:5 DES)
feed_streams.reasoning: Dipolarophile dissolved in DES; kept separate from nitrile oxide precursor until mixing point to minimize premature nitrile oxide dimerization at room temperature

reagents: ethyl nitroacetate (2.0 equiv, 1.0 M in TBAB/EG 1:5 DES)
feed_streams.reasoning: Nitrile oxide precursor dissolved in DES; 2.0 equiv used to ensure full conversion of phenylacetylene

reagents.name: phenylacetylene
reagents.notes: Terminal alkyne acting as 2π component in [3+2] cycloaddition

reagents.name: ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates nitrile oxide (ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide) in situ via thermal dehydration; excess used to drive conversion

reagents.name: tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.role: deep eutectic solvent component (hydrogen bond acceptor)
reagents.notes: Forms DES with ethylene glycol; acts as reaction medium, may also promote nitrile oxide formation

reagents.name: ethylene glycol (EG)
reagents.role: deep eutectic solvent component (hydrogen bond donor)
reagents.notes: Hydrogen bond donor in DES formation

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal dehydration/tautomerization of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide (nitrile oxide intermediate) in situ. The nitro group undergoes aci-form tautomerization followed by loss of water.

Concerted [3+2] cycloaddition of the nitrile oxide 1,3-dipole with phenylacetylene (dipolarophile) to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate regioselectively. The regioselectivity places the phenyl group at C-5 of the isoxazole.

stream_logic.reasoning: Dipolarophile in DES; separated from nitrile oxide precursor to prevent premature reaction or nitrile oxide dimerization before reaching heated zone
Nitrile oxide precursor in DES; kept separate until T-mixer immediately before heated coil to ensure nitrile oxide is generated only in the presence of the dipolarophile

chemistry_plan.mixing_order_reasoning: Streams A and B should be combined at a T-mixer immediately before the heated coil reactor. This ensures that the transient nitrile oxide intermediate generated from ethyl nitroacetate at 120°C is immediately trapped by phenylacetylene, minimizing nitrile oxide dimerization to furoxan. The DES is viscous (~50-100 cP at RT) so preheating lines to ~60°C may be needed for pumpability, but streams must not reach 120°C before mixing.
chemistry_plan.deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species or radicals involved; thermal pericyclic mechanism is unaffected by O2.
chemistry_plan.quench_reasoning: Reaction reaches full conversion in 15 min at 120°C; cooling the outlet stream to room temperature effectively stops the reaction. Batch protocol quenches with water for workup purposes only.
chemistry_plan.wavelength_reasoning: This is a purely thermal reaction; no photocatalyst or photochemical activation is involved.
chemistry_plan.confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism: well-established nitrile oxide generation from nitroacetate and [3+2] cycloaddition. Moderate uncertainty about DES pumpability in flow — TBAB/EG (1:5) is viscous at room temperature and may require heated feed lines or dilution. The 120°C temperature in a pressurized flow system should be straightforward with back-pressure regulator (BPR) to prevent boiling. The 2.0 equiv of ethyl nitroacetate suggests nitrile oxide dimerization is competitive, reinforcing the need for rapid mixing in flow. Residence time of ~15 min at 120°C is well-suited for a coil reactor.

analogies.summary: This photocycloaddition reaction utilizes triplet sensitization with 2,2'-dimethoxythioxanthone (2,2'-MeOTX) as the photocatalyst at 1 mol% loading under 455 nm COB LED irradiation. The flow process operates in acetonitrile at 200°C with a 5-minute residence time and 6.0 mL/min flow rate using a FlowSyn steel coil reactor (30 mL volume) equipped with a 34 bar back-pressure regulator. Flow chemistry enabled daisy-chaining of the initial photochemical step with high-temperature thermal cascade reactions, dramatically improving yield from 16% in batch (2 hours) to 88% in flow with a 24-fold intensification factor. The high-temperature operation required for the thermal cascade was much safer to implement in the continuous flow reactor compared to batch processing.

chemistry.reaction_name: [2 + 2] photocycloaddition followed by thermal electrocyclic cascade
chemistry.bond_formed: C-C bonds in cyclobutane ring and subsequent cascade products
chemistry.scope_summary: Five aryl enamide derivatives tested (phenyl, 3-F, 3-Br, 3-OMe, 4-CF3) with yields of 63-68% for most substrates. The 4-CF3 derivative was resistant to the thermal fragmentation step.

optimization_table.key_findings: MeCN solvent with 0.5 M concentration at 200°C with 5 min residence time gave optimal results

figures.key_information: photochemical reactor setup with dual light sources positioned above a round bottom flask on a heating plate

The ease at which this could be purified by filtration represents one of the main benefits of acetonitrile over xylene, which would not be possible without the use of continuous flow technology. This result represents a 24 h productivity of 490 g. translation_logic.batch_limitation: Low yield (16%) and long reaction time (120 min) in batch mode
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Daisy-chaining photochemistry with high-temperature thermal cascade reactions enabled by flow technology, achieving high productivity
translation_logic.what_changed: Temperature increased to 200°C, residence time reduced to 5 min, back pressure of 34 bar applied
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Solvent (MeCN), concentration (0.5 M), light source power (36 W)
translation_logic.safety_improvement: High temperature thermal cascade reactions safer in flow than batch

figure_descriptions: Reactor scheme showing photochemical setup with light sources
Flow schematic for daisy-chained photo/thermal process

full_record.notes: High confidence extraction with clear quantitative data from optimization tables. Flow process shows dramatic improvement in yield (16% to 88%) and time reduction (120 min to 5 min). Productivity of 20 g/h mentioned in key claim.

analogies.summary: This process demonstrates a [3 + 2] dipolar cycloaddition reaction conducted in a continuous flow unit (CFU) with automated pumps. The reaction uses DMSO as solvent with diisopropylamine as base, operating at 60°C with a 20-minute residence time at 0.165 mL/min flow rate, achieving 96% yield. The flow process allows for higher temperature operation (60°C vs 20°C in batch) while maintaining excellent yield, with the batch baseline achieving 100% yield at room temperature in the same reaction time. The continuous flow setup enables better temperature control and process intensification, allowing the reaction to proceed efficiently at elevated temperature with only a minimal decrease in yield compared to the batch process.

chemistry.scope_summary: The process shows a wide scope for synthesizing 1-aryl-1,2,3-triazoles from various anilines, including electron-rich and electron-poor substrates, yielding triazoles efficiently in good yields.

optimization_table.key_findings: CuI (10 mol%) with 1,10-phenanthroline (10 mol%) at 60°C for 20 min gave 100% conversion.

full_record.notes: Flow process achieves high yield with optimized conditions, BPR inferred from reactor scheme.

analogies.record_id: Yang 등 - 2024 - Safety Guided Process Development and Scale-Up of the Highly Energetic Compound 4-Methyl-1,2,5-oxadi.pdf
analogies.summary: This process involves the synthesis of 4-methyl-1,2,5-oxadiazole, a highly energetic compound, using a nitration or cyclization reaction in a concentrated sulfuric acid/water mixture (63:6.3 ratio). The flow process was conducted in a tubular reactor at 154°C with a residence time of 179 minutes (approximately 3 hours) and a flow rate of 0.12 mL/min, requiring a back-pressure regulator at 47.6 bar to maintain liquid phase conditions. Flow chemistry enabled a significant temperature increase from 65°C in batch to 154°C, resulting in an improved yield from 70.8% to 78.8%. The primary advantage of flow over batch was enhanced safety when handling this highly energetic compound, as the continuous process allows for better control of the exothermic reaction and reduces the inventory of hazardous material at any given time. The high pressure and temperature conditions achievable in the flow reactor also contributed to the improved reaction efficiency.

additives.name: N-methyl morpholine
additives.role: forms stable NMM salt of the product

optimization_table.key_findings: Increasing the residence time to 180 min and temperature to 157 °C resulted in complete conversion and >99% product purity.

assumptions: D = 1e-09 m²/s (typical for small organic molecules in liquid)

steps.summary: Q_gen = 0.4430 W, Q_rem = 360.0001 W, Da_th = 0.0012 — Thermally safe

steps.name: Back-Pressure Regulator
steps.summary: BPR REQUIRED: 5.0 bar (Gas-liquid system detected — BPR mandatory to maintain gas solubility (Henry's law: C_gas = H·P) and prevent uncontrolled degassing.)
equations: C_{\mathrm{gas}} = H \cdot P_{\mathrm{partial}}\quad\text{(higher BPR} \Rightarrow \text{more dissolved gas)}

steps.name: Process Metrics
steps.summary: STY = 0.4150 mol/(L·h), P = 12.450 mmol/h, ṅ_lim = 0.0333 mmol/min, startup waste = 90.0 mL, IF = 0×

streams.reasoning: From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: dipolarophile in DES, separated from nitrile oxide precursor to prevent premature reaction. Concentration matches batch protocol.
From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: nitrile oxide precursor in DES, kept separate until T-mixer. Higher concentration (1.0 M vs 0.5 M) to deliver 2.0 equiv in smaller volume.

proposal.mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: Streams A and B combined at T-mixer immediately before heated coil reactor. This ensures transient nitrile oxide intermediate generated from ethyl nitroacetate at 120°C is immediately trapped by phenylacetylene, minimizing nitrile oxide dimerization to furoxan.
pre_reactor_steps: Preheat feed lines to 60°C to improve DES pumpability (viscosity ~50-100 cP at RT)
post_reactor_steps: Cool outlet stream to room temperature to stop reaction
proposal.chemistry_notes: Thermal 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition with in-situ nitrile oxide generation. Key design: separate streams prevent premature nitrile oxide formation/dimerization. DES viscosity requires preheated feed lines. No photochemistry involved - purely thermal mechanism. BPR required due to high temperature (120°C) and potential water generation from nitrile oxide formation. Calculations show kinetically controlled regime (Da = 0.492) with excellent plug flow (Pe = 691).

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Calculation estimated 60.0 min (range 1.0-90.0 min) via analogy. Analogy 2 used 20 min for [3+2] cycloaddition but at lower temperature (60°C vs 120°C). Analogy 3 used 179 min at 154°C but different mechanism. Using calculation result of 60 min as it accounts for our specific kinetics.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Calculation: total flow = reactor_volume/residence_time = 30.0 mL / 60.0 min = 0.5 mL/min. Stream A: 0.167 mL/min, Stream B: 0.333 mL/min to deliver proper stoichiometry.
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Same as batch (120°C) - Chemistry Plan indicates this temperature required for nitrile oxide generation. No adjustment needed.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Calculation result: 0.067 M in reactor after mixing. Lower than batch (0.5 M) due to flow dilution but sufficient for reaction.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation required 5.0 bar. High temperature (120°C) and potential water generation from nitrile oxide formation necessitates BPR to maintain liquid phase.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: PFA chosen for high temperature compatibility (120°C) and chemical resistance to DES. Not photochemistry so transparency not required.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: Calculation used 1.0 mm giving Re = 10.6 (laminar flow), Pe = 691 (excellent plug flow), reasonable pressure drop (0.13 bar).
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: Calculation result: 30.0 mL. Matches Analogy 1 reactor volume, appropriate for 60 min residence time.
reasoning_per_field.light_setup: Empty string - Chemistry Plan confirms no photochemistry involved, purely thermal mechanism.
reasoning_per_field.wavelength_nm: Null - no light required for thermal cycloaddition reaction.
